---
title: "Ovarian Cyst Analysis Plots"
author: "Mirra Chinta"
date: "2026-03-19"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: scroll
    theme: journal
    social: menu
runtime: shiny
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(flexdashboard)
library(shiny)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(plotly)
library(broom)
library(splines)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
```

```{r}
#chq <- read_excel("C:/Users/mirra/OneDrive/Documents/MHQuestionnaires/CHQ_dataset_040925.xlsx")
#OV_data <- read_excel("C:/Users/mirra/OneDrive/Documents/PBBThesis/Ovcysts_dataset_110425.xlsx")
chq <- read_excel("data/CHQ_dataset_040925.xlsx")
OV_data <- read_excel("data/Ovcysts_dataset_110425.xlsx")

OV_data <- OV_data %>%
  rename(INDIVIDUAL_ID = Individual_ID) %>%
  left_join(
    chq %>% select(INDIVIDUAL_ID, his_preg),
    by = "INDIVIDUAL_ID"
  ) %>%
  rename(CHQ_his_preg = his_preg)
#picking 2017 values for people with both date blood samples
logfunc <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x) | x <= 0, NA_real_, log(x))
df <- OV_data %>%
  mutate(
    PBB_final = coalesce(PBB153_S2017, PBB153_S2012),
    PCB_final = coalesce(SumPCB3_S2017, SumPCB3_S2012),
    lipid_final = coalesce(lipids_S2017, lipids_S2012),
    int_age_final = coalesce(CHQ_intage, GHQ_intage),
    Ovarian_Cyst_bin = case_when(
      coalesce(CHQ_repro_w_dr_ovarcyst, GHQ_ovar_cyst) == 1 ~ "Yes",
      coalesce(CHQ_repro_w_dr_ovarcyst, GHQ_ovar_cyst) == 0 ~ "No",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    y = case_when(
      Ovarian_Cyst_bin == "Yes" ~ 1,
      Ovarian_Cyst_bin == "No" ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
#form tertiles
tertPBB <- quantile(df$PBB_final, probs = c(1/3, 2/3), na.rm = TRUE)
tertPCB <- quantile(df$PCB_final, probs = c(1/3, 2/3), na.rm = TRUE)
#put tertiles of exposure in data frame
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    PBB_cat = case_when(
      is.na(PBB_final) ~ NA_character_,
      PBB_final <= tertPBB[1] ~ "Low",
      PBB_final <= tertPBB[2] ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ "High"
    ),
    PCB_cat = case_when(
      is.na(PCB_final) ~ NA_character_,
      PCB_final <= tertPCB[1] ~ "Low",
      PCB_final <= tertPCB[2] ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ "High"
    ),
    PBB_cat = factor(PBB_cat, levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High")),
    PCB_cat = factor(PCB_cat, levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High"))
  )
```

```{r}
selectInput(
  "forest_exposure",
  "Forest plot exposure:",
  choices = c("PBB", "PCB"),
  selected = "PBB"
)

selectInput(
  "spline_exposure",
  "Spline plot exposure:",
  choices = c("PBB", "PCB"),
  selected = "PBB"
)
```


Dataset Description
=======================================================================

Row
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Data Source

This dashboard uses ovarian cyst outcome data from the Comprehensive Health Questionnaire and the General Health Questionnaire, with covariates like BMI, parity, age at menarche, and interview age.The Comprehesive Health Questionnaire was distributed between 2017-2022, and the General Health Questionnaire was distributed between 2012-2015 with participants older than 18. The sample size was 396 participants with a PBB blood level and had reported yes or no to the ovarian cyst question in either the CHQ or GHQ. Exposure measures include PBB-153 and summed PCB values, prioritizing 2017 serum values when available and otherwise using 2012 values.

### Sample Size

```{r}
renderUI({
  HTML(paste0(
    "<b>Total dataset:</b> ", nrow(df), " participants<br>",
    "<b>With ovarian cyst outcome:</b> ", sum(!is.na(df$y)), "<br>",
    "<b>PBB analytic sample:</b> ", sum(!is.na(df$y) & !is.na(df$PBB_final) & !is.na(df$int_age_final) & !is.na(df$lipid_final)), "<br>",
    "<b>PCB analytic sample:</b> ", sum(!is.na(df$y) & !is.na(df$PCB_final) & !is.na(df$int_age_final) & !is.na(df$lipid_final))
  ))
})


```

Analysis
=======================================================================
Inputs {.sidebar data-width=450}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
selectInput(
  inputId = "forest_exposure",
  label = "Forest plot exposure:",
  choices = c("PBB", "PCB"),
  selected = "PBB"
)

selectInput(
  inputId = "spline_exposure",
  label = "Spline plot exposure:",
  choices = c("PBB", "PCB"),
  selected = "PBB"
)
```

Forest Plot
```{r}
renderPlotly({

  if (input$forest_exposure == "PBB") {
    data <- df %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(y),
        !is.na(PBB_cat),
        !is.na(int_age_final),
        !is.na(lipid_final)
      )
#adjust for age and lipids
    fit <- glm(
      y ~ PBB_cat + int_age_final + lipid_final,
      family = poisson(link = "log"),
      data = data
    )
    robust <- lmtest::coeftest(
      fit,
      vcov = sandwich::vcovHC(fit, type = "HC0")
    )

    forest_df <- data.frame(
      term = rownames(robust),
      estimate = robust[, 1],
      se = robust[, 2],
      p.value = robust[, 4]
    ) %>%
      filter(grepl("^PBB_cat", term)) %>%
      mutate(
        comparison = case_when(
          term == "PBB_catModerate" ~ "Moderate vs Low",
          term == "PBB_catHigh" ~ "High vs Low"
        ),
        PR = exp(estimate),
        lowCI = exp(estimate - 1.96 * se),
        highCI = exp(estimate + 1.96 * se),
        significant = ifelse(lowCI > 1 | highCI < 1, "Significant", "Not significant"),
        hover = paste0(
          "<b>", comparison, "</b>",
          "<br>PR: ", round(PR, 2),
          "<br>95% CI: ", round(lowCI, 2), " to ", round(highCI, 2),
          "<br>p-value: ", signif(p.value, 2)
        )
      )

    title_text <- "Forest Plot PRs: PBB"
  } else {
    #PCB forest
    data <- df %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(y),
        !is.na(PCB_cat),
        !is.na(int_age_final),
        !is.na(lipid_final)
      )

    fit <- glm(
      y ~ PCB_cat + int_age_final + lipid_final,
      family = poisson(link = "log"),
      data = data
    )

    robust <- lmtest::coeftest(
      fit,
      vcov = sandwich::vcovHC(fit, type = "HC0")
    )

    forest_df <- data.frame(
      term = rownames(robust),
      estimate = robust[, 1],
      se = robust[, 2],
      p.value = robust[, 4]
    ) %>%
      filter(grepl("^PCB_cat", term)) %>%
      mutate(
        comparison = case_when(
          term == "PCB_catModerate" ~ "Moderate vs Low",
          term == "PCB_catHigh" ~ "High vs Low"
        ),
        PR = exp(estimate),
        lowCI = exp(estimate - 1.96 * se),
        highCI = exp(estimate + 1.96 * se),
        significant = ifelse(lowCI > 1 | highCI < 1, "Significant", "Not significant"),
        hover = paste0(
          "<b>", comparison, "</b>",
          "<br>PR: ", round(PR, 2),
          "<br>95% CI: ", round(lowCI, 2), " to ", round(highCI, 2),
          "<br>p-value: ", signif(p.value, 2)
        )
      )

    title_text <- "Forest Plot PRs: PCB"
  }

  forest_df$comparison <- factor(
    forest_df$comparison,
    levels = c("High vs Low", "Moderate vs Low")
  )

  plot_ly(
    data = forest_df,
    x = ~PR,
    y = ~comparison,
    type = "scatter",
    mode = "markers",
    text = ~hover,
    hoverinfo = "text",
    color = ~significant,
    colors = c("Not significant" = "blue", "Significant" = "red"),
    marker = list(size = 10)
  ) %>%
    add_segments(
      data = forest_df,
      x = ~lowCI,
      xend = ~highCI,
      y = ~comparison,
      yend = ~comparison,
      inherit = FALSE,
      line = list(color = "black"),
      showlegend = FALSE
    ) %>%
    layout(
      title = list(
        text = paste0(
          title_text,
          "<br><sup>Adjusted for age and lipids</sup>"
        )
      ),
      xaxis = list(
  title = "Prevalence Ratio",
  range = c(0, max(forest_df$highCI, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1)
      ),
      yaxis = list(title = ""),
      shapes = list(
        list(
          type = "line",
          x0 = 1,
          x1 = 1,
          y0 = -0.5,
          y1 = 1.5,
          line = list(dash = "dash")
        )
      ),
      margin = list(l = 110, r = 20, t = 70, b = 50)
    )
})
```

Spline Plot
```{r}
renderPlotly({

  if (input$spline_exposure == "PBB") {
    data <- df %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(y),
        !is.na(PBB_final),
        !is.na(int_age_final),
        !is.na(lipid_final)
      )
#three knots
    fit <- glm(
      y ~ ns(PBB_final, df = 3) + int_age_final + lipid_final,
      family = poisson(link = "log"),
      data = data
    )
#5th to 95th percentiles
    xseq <- seq(
      quantile(data$PBB_final, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      quantile(data$PBB_final, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
      length.out = 200
    )

    newdat <- data.frame(
      PBB_final = xseq,
      int_age_final = median(data$int_age_final, na.rm = TRUE),
      lipid_final = median(data$lipid_final, na.rm = TRUE)
    )

    pred <- predict(fit, newdata = newdat, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)

    spline_df <- newdat %>%
      mutate(
        x = PBB_final,
        fit = exp(pred$fit),
        lowCI = exp(pred$fit - 1.96 * pred$se.fit),
        highCI = exp(pred$fit + 1.96 * pred$se.fit),
        hover = paste0(
          "<b>PBB</b>",
          "<br>Exposure: ", round(x, 2),
          "<br>Predicted prevalence: ", round(fit, 3),
          "<br>95% CI: ", round(lowCI, 2), " to ", round(highCI, 2)
        )
      )

    xlab <- "PBB (ug/mL)"
    title_text <- "Spline Plot: PBB"

  } else {
    data <- df %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(y),
        !is.na(PCB_final),
        !is.na(int_age_final),
        !is.na(lipid_final)
      )

    fit <- glm(
      y ~ ns(PCB_final, df = 3) + int_age_final + lipid_final,
      family = poisson(link = "log"),
      data = data
    )

    xseq <- seq(
      quantile(data$PCB_final, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      quantile(data$PCB_final, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
      length.out = 200
    )

    newdat <- data.frame(
      PCB_final = xseq,
      int_age_final = median(data$int_age_final, na.rm = TRUE),
      lipid_final = median(data$lipid_final, na.rm = TRUE)
    )

    pred <- predict(fit, newdata = newdat, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)

    spline_df <- newdat %>%
      mutate(
        x = PCB_final,
        fit = exp(pred$fit),
        lowCI = exp(pred$fit - 1.96 * pred$se.fit),
        highCI = exp(pred$fit + 1.96 * pred$se.fit),
        hover = paste0(
          "<b>PCB</b>",
          "<br>Exposure: ", round(x, 2),
          "<br>Predicted prevalence: ", round(fit, 3),
          "<br>95% CI: ", round(lowCI, 2), " to ", round(highCI, 2)
        )
      )

    xlab <- "PCB (ug/mL)"
    title_text <- "Spline Plot: PCB"
  }

  plot_ly(data = spline_df, x = ~x) %>%
    add_ribbons(
      ymin = ~lowCI,
      ymax = ~highCI,
      line = list(color = "transparent"),
      fillcolor = "rgba(100,100,100,0.2)",
      hoverinfo = "none",
      name = "95% CI"
    ) %>%
    add_lines(
      y = ~fit,
      text = ~hover,
      hoverinfo = "text",
      line = list(color = "black", width = 3),
      name = "Spline"
    ) %>%
    layout(
      title = list(
        text = paste0(
          title_text,
          "<br><sup>Adjusted for age and lipids</sup>"
        )
      ),
      xaxis = list(title = xlab),
      yaxis = list(title = "Predicted prevalence"),
      margin = list(l = 70, r = 20, t = 70, b = 50)
    )
})
```
Row {data-height=180}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### Forest Plot Takeaway {data-width=50}
```{r}
renderUI({
  HTML(paste0(
    "<b>Takeaway:</b> This forest plot shows the age and lipids adjusted association between ",
    input$forest_exposure,
    " tertiles and ovarian cyst prevalence. ",
    "Each point shows the prevalence ratio of ovarian cysts comparing either moderate to low or high to low, as well as the CI and p-value. ",
    "Estimates in red are statistically significant."
  ))
})
```
### Spline Plot Takeaway {data-width=50}
```{r}
renderUI({
  HTML(paste0(
    "<b>Takeaway:</b> This spline plot shows the potential nonlinear relationships between ",
    input$spline_exposure,
    "exposure and predicted ovarian cyst prevalence after adjustment for age and lipids. ",
    "The shaded area shows the CI."
  ))
})
```
Row {data-height=180}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### Real World Takeaway {data-width=50}
```{r}
renderUI({
  HTML(paste0(
    "<b>Takeaway:</b> Ovarian cyst prevalence was a major health concern among women in the PBB Registry. Understanding whether PBB and PCB exposure could be associated with increased prevalence of ovarian cysts can lead to increased awareness of what to ask doctors for when going in for annual check-ins and can also improve physician awareness of ovarian health with respect to chemical exposures."
  ))
})
```
Row {data-height=180}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### Github link {data-width=50}
```{r}
renderUI({
  HTML(paste0(
    "https://github.com/mchintaemory/OvarianCystDashboard"
  ))
})

```
